title: Particulate matter detector and method for detecting particulate matter
abstract: A particulate matter detector (1) a first, a second and a third waveguide (11, 12, 13), the third waveguide (13) being free of cladding, and a waveguide splitter (14). The first waveguide (11) is coupled to a light emitter (10) and comprises an interrogation region (16) formed by a cladding- free surface of the first waveguide (11), the surface being exposed to a gaseous environment (2) and configured to accumulate directly thereon particulate matter (3) from the gaseous environment (2). During a measurement phase, a first intensity of light in the first waveguide (11) is set for determining a change in the intensity of the light detected by the detector (15) in dependence of accumulated particulate matter (3). During a cleaning phase, a second intensity larger than the first intensity is set for directing the accumulated particulate matter (3) from the interrogation region (16) to the third waveguide (13) via optical forces.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A particulate matter detector (1), comprising: - a light emitter (10) configured to emit light; - a first, a second and a third waveguide (11, 12, 13), the third waveguide (13) being free of cladding; - a waveguide splitter (14); - a detector (15); and - a controller (20); - wherein the first waveguide (11) is coupled to the light emitter (10) and is configured to guide the emitted light towards the waveguide splitter (14), the first waveguide (11) comprising an interrogation region (16) formed by a cladding-free surface of the first waveguide (11), the surface being exposed to a gaseous environment (2) and configured to accumulate directly thereon particulate matter (3) from the gaseous environment (2); - wherein the waveguide splitter (14) couples the interrogation region (16) of the first waveguide (11) to the second waveguide (12) and to the third waveguide (13); - wherein the detector (15) is coupled to the second waveguide (12) and configured to detect an intensity of light that is split from the interrogation region (16) into the second waveguide (12) by the waveguide splitter (14); and - wherein the controller (20) is configured to - during a measurement phase, set a first intensity of the light coupled into the first waveguide (11), determine a change in the intensity of the light detected by the detector (15), and output an indication of an opacity of the surface of the first waveguide (11) with the 

 accumulated particulate matter (3) thereon based on the determined change; and - during a cleaning phase, set a second intensity of the light coupled into the first waveguide (11) that is larger than the first intensity for directing the accumulated particulate matter (3) from the interrogation region (16) to the third waveguide (13) via optical forces. 2. The particulate matter detector (1) according to claim 1, wherein the controller (20) determines the indication of the opacity by comparing the detected intensity with the first intensity. 3. The particulate matter detector (1) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the first waveguide (11) is coupled to the light emitter (10) via a first grating coupler (17); and/or the second waveguide (12) is coupled to the detector (15) via a second grating coupler (18). 4. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 3, wherein a length of the third waveguide (13) is larger than a length of the second waveguide (12). 5. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the third waveguide (13) forms a spiral or a meander trap. 6. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the third waveguide (13) at least in some regions is thinned down. 7. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first waveguide (11) at least in a portion of the interrogation region (16) is thinned down. 8. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 7, further comprising an enclosure (21) that covers at least a portion of the third waveguide (13). 9. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 8, further comprising means to apply electrophoresis and/or thermophoresis to at least a portion of the third waveguide (13). 10. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the second waveguide (12) comprises a cladding (19). 11. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the waveguide splitter (14) is one of: a passive waveguide splitter having a fixed energy splitting ratio, wherein a higher energy portion is split from the interrogation region (16) into the third waveguide (13); a selective waveguide splitter characterized by a polarization or wavelength dependent energy splitting ratio; and an active waveguide splitter operable to selectively couple light from the interrogation region (16) into the second and third waveguide (12, 13). 12. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 11, wherein during the measurement phase the 

controller (20) is further configured to output an indication of a particle number and/or size of the accumulated particulate matter (3) based on the determined change. 13. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the first waveguide (11) in the interrogation region (16) and/or the second waveguide (12) comprises a thin-film coating, in particular a thin-film Parylene or PTFE coating. 14. The particulate matter detector (1) according to one of claims 1 to 13, further comprising a waveguide combiner (14a) and a further light emitter (10a); wherein the light emitter (10) is configured to provide the first intensity and the further light emitter (10a) is configured to provide the second intensity; and the waveguide combiner (14a) couples the light emitter (10) and the further light emitter (10a) to the first waveguide (11). 15. A method for detecting particulate matter (3), the method comprising: during a measurement phase: - accumulating particulate matter (3) from a gaseous environment (2) directly on a cladding-free surface of a first waveguide (11) within an interrogation region (16); - guiding light from a light emitter (10) through the first waveguide (11) to a waveguide splitter (14) at a first intensity; - splitting by means of the waveguide splitter (14) at least a portion of the light from the first waveguide 

 (11) into a second waveguide (12) that is coupled to a detector (15); - detecting an intensity of light within the second waveguide (12) and determining a change in the intensity of the light detected by the detector (15); and - outputting an indication of an opacity of the surface of the first waveguide (11) with the accumulated particulate matter (3) thereon based on the determined change; and - during a cleaning phase: - guiding light from the light emitter (10) through the first waveguide (11) to the waveguide splitter (14) at a second intensity that is larger than the first intensity; - splitting by means of the waveguide splitter (14) at least a portion of the light from the first waveguide (11) into a third waveguide (13) that is free of cladding (19); and - directing the accumulated particulate matter (3) from the interrogation region (16) to the third waveguide (13) via optical forces.
